mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment above a crisis threshold signals weak aggregate demand and depressed corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the medium term as the recovery stalls.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-GFC recovery is characterized by a 'jobless' recovery narrative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE level is above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: In a Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP) environment, falling inflation (disinflation) increases real interest rates, tightening financial conditions unexpectedly and negatively impacting equity valuations which are sensitive to discount rates.
failure_condition: A negative 3-month change in CPIAUCSL is not followed by negative S&P 500 returns on average over the next 20 days when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales is a direct, high-frequency indicator of consumer health. This strength in consumption translates into higher expected corporate revenues and drives positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding economic activity and supply-side strength. This serves as a leading indicator for corporate profits in the goods-producing sector, supporting positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key input for business cycle dating.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO has a Rank IC below 0.03 when predicting 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rapid increase in the unemployment rate and outright job losses (negative payroll growth) is a robust, real-time indicator of recession, causing a sharp decline in equity prices as investors price in a severe downturn.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500's average 60-day forward return is not statistically different from zero or is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a key long-leading cyclical indicator. A level below 1 million units signals a deep contraction in the housing sector with negative spillover effects on banking and consumption, depressing broad market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing market collapse was the epicenter of the 2008 financial crisis.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not statistically lower in periods where HOUST is below 1000 compared to periods when it is above.

mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free rate used to discount future corporate earnings in valuation models. This directly increases the present value of equities, providing a short-term tailwind to prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The Equity Risk Premium (ERP) is measured relative to a risk-free rate.
failure_condition: The correlation between the level of FEDFUNDS and S&P 500 forward 5-day returns is positive or zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary environment, strong job creation is the most critical signal for a sustainable recovery. A positive surprise in nonfarm payrolls reduces economic uncertainty and drives equity markets higher.
evidence_refs: fact:The sustainability of the post-2009 recovery is a primary market concern.
failure_condition: Instances where the monthly change in PAYEMS exceeds 150,000 are not followed by a positive average 20-day S&P 500 return.
